title: polyalthia hirta
intro: Polyalthia hirta, also known as the Pala Tree or Indian Walnut, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Polyalthia hirta include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the tree are often used to make decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are consumed orally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
